pages.title: Overview
pages.subtitle: Four surface families, one semantic spine.
pages.hero_statement: Taxat exposes collaboration, portal, governance, and native operator embodiments through shared shell, continuity, selector, and recovery law.

pages.title: Collaboration
pages.subtitle: Staff and customer lanes over the same workflow truth.
pages.hero_statement: The collaboration family keeps one work object legible across queue triage, workspace threads, request lists, and customer-safe request detail without leaking internal-only context.

pages.title: Portal
pages.subtitle: Uploads, approvals, onboarding, and help stay plain-language and recoverable.
pages.hero_statement: The portal family turns northbound truth into customer-safe steps, request-safe upload states, and in-route trust moments that do not require internal product literacy.

pages.title: Governance
pages.subtitle: Diff-first change staging with audit-native visibility.
pages.hero_statement: The governance family manages tenant policy, access, authority links, retention, and investigations through basis-hash-aware projections and staged mutations.

pages.title: Native
pages.subtitle: Primary scenes, support windows, command surfaces, and restore envelopes.
pages.hero_statement: The macOS workspace makes operator depth native with split views, keyboard focus grammar, detached support windows, and strict scene-restoration legality.

pages.title: Continuity & Recovery
pages.subtitle: Route restoration, stream resume, focus return, and reduced-state taxonomy across all families.
pages.hero_statement: Every family shares the same recovery vocabulary: keep valid content mounted, explain stale posture inline, and return the user to the narrowest surviving anchor.

surface_families.label: Collaboration Workspace
surface_families.thesis: A shared work object exposes distinct staff and customer views without ever crossing visibility lanes or losing queue continuity.
surface_families.interaction_signature: Queue -> workspace -> thread/module focus with inline rebase and append-only activity.
surface_families.promoted_support_region_law: DETAIL_DRAWER in staff surfaces; contextual support panel in customer-safe request views.

surface_families.label: Client Portal
surface_families.thesis: A task-first, customer-safe portal keeps language plain, support subordinate, and trust moments explicit across upload, approval, onboarding, and help routes.
surface_families.interaction_signature: Single-column primary task flow with contextual history, help, and artifact handoff kept subordinate.
surface_families.promoted_support_region_law: Exactly one support region outside Help; help route itself may foreground support context.

surface_families.label: Governance Console
surface_families.thesis: Dense control-plane work stays diff-first, basis-hash-aware, and audit-visible without collapsing into noisy admin chrome.
surface_families.interaction_signature: Context bar + section nav + inventory rail + workspace canvas + audit sidecar, with staged mutation baskets and approval posture.
surface_families.promoted_support_region_law: AUDIT_SIDECAR is the default promoted support region unless a route-local support window temporarily supersedes it.

surface_families.label: macOS Operator Workspace
surface_families.thesis: The native client turns calm-shell law into multi-window operator depth, keyboard surfaces, and scene restoration without inventing new product semantics.
surface_families.interaction_signature: Primary split view scenes, parent-bound secondary windows, command-surface shortcuts, and cache-backed resume with fail-closed legality checks.
surface_families.promoted_support_region_law: Trailing inspector in primary scenes; parent-bound secondary windows for compare, audit, packet, and authority review.

shared_laws.statement: A stable object keeps the same shell family or embodiment frame across refresh, reconnect, and deep-link return.

source_refs.rationale: Cross-surface shell law is authoritative.
Native scenes embody browser shell law rather than replacing it.

shared_laws.statement: Each mounted route or scene gets at most one promoted support region by default, even when dense detail or audit context is present.

source_refs.rationale: Support promotion is capped across shell families.
Governance sidecar promotion follows the same cap.

shared_laws.statement: Previously valid content stays mounted while stale, reconnect, or limited-read posture is explained inline and mutation surfaces fail closed.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: Shared shell freshness and recovery vocabulary
source_refs.rationale: The shared taxonomy governs stale and degraded states.
Workspace delta and activity events keep the shell mounted through rebase.

shared_laws.statement: All durable product mutations travel through `POST /v1/commands`, except governed upload-session allocation for raw bytes, while route reads stay role-filtered projections.

source_refs.rationale: The northbound contract pins the command endpoint.
Portal projections stay customer-safe and role-filtered.

shared_laws.statement: Internal-only collaboration activity, masked governance slices, customer-safe portal projections, and native cached state remain explicitly partitioned and never inferred across surfaces.

source_refs.rationale: Customer-visible and internal activity are stored and streamed separately.
Visibility and masking are part of cache legality.

shared_laws.statement: Detail drawers, support panels, modals, detached windows, and external handoffs restore focus to a serialized parent anchor rather than forcing rediscovery.

source_refs.rationale: Parent focus targets are contractually preserved.
Detached native windows restore focus to the parent scene.

visual_research.title: A calmer interface for a product in motion
visual_research.takeaway: Let navigation recede so the working surface carries the strongest visual weight, and soften structure until it is felt rather than loudly seen.
visual_research.scope: Aesthetic reference only. Taxat algorithm contracts remain the sole semantic authority.

visual_research.title: Vercel Design
visual_research.takeaway: Systemize craft: restrained typography, consistent spacing, and a design-language spine strong enough to support multiple interface embodiments.
visual_research.scope: Aesthetic reference only. Used to tune the atlas visual discipline, not the product semantics.

visual_research.title: Raycast User Interface API
visual_research.takeaway: Keep native operator flows keyboard-first, with a list-detail-action-panel mental model and fast scene rendering under loading or reconnect.
visual_research.scope: Aesthetic and interaction reference only for native command-surface posture.

read_model_bindings.notes: The inbox is the staff collaboration queue root and must update in place.
This is the canonical calm-shell collaboration workspace and owns same-object/same-shell continuity for work items.
Customer-visible activity is a separate stream and cannot be contaminated by internal-only facts.
This module is the hard internal/customer boundary inside collaboration.
The files module is the collaboration truth surface for current-vs-history artifact posture.

This is the customer-safe collaboration detail route that shares portal shell law without inheriting staff-only vocabulary.
The atlas binds Home to `/portal` as the canonical root route because the contract names Home but not `/portal/home`.
Upload action families are normalized from prose because the contract names the flow and the byte-session exception, not a complete command enum.
Approval command names are normalized from route-local action prose rather than a published command enum.
Onboarding continuity is route-local and must not be reinterpreted as a general portal dashboard state.
Help is the only portal route where the support surface is allowed to foreground itself.
Overview keeps the five-region governance grammar visible even when density collapses responsively.
Mutation families are normalized from the governance route prose; the northbound contract instead pins stale guards and basis hashes.
This route preserves the governance five-layer access explanation stack in detail form.
The roles route is dense but never allowed to hide diff and blast-radius posture behind collapsible noise.
The simulator route is the most explicit place where governance basis hashes become user-visible product law.
Authority link health categories stay deliberately distinct to prevent semantic flattening in later UI work.
This route uses a normalized landmark order because the contract names the workspace more strongly than exact landmark sequencing.
Legal holds remain explicitly distinct from broader retention policy edits.
This route is the clearest example of the governance stale-view hard stop on irreversible action.
This route is read-heavy and append-only, but still governed by the same return-target and support-region laws.
Native primary scenes embody calm-shell law; they do not define a fourth shell family.
The work-item scene is the native mirror of the collaboration workspace, with native command affordances layered on top.
